steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is B, $14x+168$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Distribute
steps.content: Multiply $14$ by each term inside the parentheses: $$14(x+12)=14x+168$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: The correct answer is $14x+168$, so B is correct.

steps.title: Avoid the distribution trap
steps.content: The $14$ must multiply both terms inside the parentheses. One incorrect answer leaves the $12$ unchanged, and the other choices use constants that do not equal $14\cdot 12$.
